answer:
Maskull realizes he is actually **Nightspore**, the friend whose acquaintance Krag had invited him to meet.

This revelation comes after he learns of his own impending death and encounters Krag again, uncovering the cyclical nature of his identity tied to the philosophical and existential themes of the story.

The twist underscores the narrative’s critique of self-perception and the illusory nature of individuality within the author’s Gnostic-inspired framework.
